Patch Set 3:

(7 comments)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Patch is a huge improvement over the current code (< 100 credits per stripe vs. > 200 credits per stripe before).

Some minor improvements are possible before landing this (needs retesting anyway due to LU-4420 failure).

With LFSCK Phase 2 landing, I wonder if we could allow OST orphan objects to be cleaned up by LFSCK in case of failure during create?  Maybe not have it complain about orphan objects that are zero size and still have SUID|SGID flags set ?  That would allow dropping all the llog records for each stripe, refucing the per-stripe overhead significantly.

It would be nice if the commit comment showed the before and after summary of the declared transaction operations/credits, so we can see what is still consuming the test of the space.

Patch Set 4:

I think write declaration can be improved to take current tree depth into account. we do use regular writes for tiny files like LAST_RCVD and llogs. with 64b records a plain llog can grow upto 4MB which is 1024 4K blocks, so we'd need 3 indirect blocks to cover 4MB or just a single level of indirection. to allocate a block we need to modify bitmap+gdp. so,  for 4MB file the metadata is 3 indirect blocks to allocate (3*2) + modify them (3) + inode itself (1) = 10 at most, which less then current:
         /**
         * credits for single block write.
         */
        [DTO_WRITE_BLOCK]   = 14,

I tried this locally and got:
before - write: 51/370, after - write: 51/278

Patch Set 13:

Alex, when rebasing a patch, please still mark the previous test failures against a Jira ticket.  Either the failures are caused by the patch and should be fixed in the patch, or the failures are not in the patch and should be blamed on some other ticket and be fixed.  If we just ignore test failures until the patch passes then the code will get in continually worse shape until nothing ever passes.

Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

Looking through the previous failures, I see that conf-sanity.sh test_32a has had a timeout five times for this patch in the past four weeks, but no other patches have failed that test in that time.  I haven't looked at all the other failures, but that one at least appears to be caused by this patch even if it doesn't appear to be related.

parents.subject: LU-4624 statahead: Improve statahead debug messages

commit.subject: LU-4611 osd: improve credits calculation
commit.message:
LU-4611 osd: improve credits calculation

- llog catalog do not declare records twice (for old and new objects)
  this might be an issue on ZFS with full debug enabled..
- llog to specify append by pos=-1, so OSD can take this into account
- object create/destroy should not include OI, this is calculated yet
- EA declaration to improve few specific cases
- osd_declare_write() to recognize overwrite optimistically,
  using inode size and i_blocks
- osd_declare_write() to optimize very specific cases, like legacy
  blockmaps with small offsets and allocated indirects
- index delete modify just a single block

preliminary testing on a local setup with 7 OSTs:
1360 credits before and 436 credits after.

llog declarations and index inserts (part of llog object creation)
still consume about 70% (7 OSTs):
   create: 7/28, destroy: 1/4
   attr_set: 2/2, xattr_set: 8/21
   write: 37/179, punch: 14/56, quota 2/2
   insert: 8/135, delete: 2/5
   ref_add: 1/1, ref_del: 3/3
